s1_reasoning: The kernel performs ~2 flops per inner-k iteration but streams A[k][i] and B[k][j] from memory with poor temporal locality for A (column access in row-major) and limited reuse of B[k][j] across i. Arithmetic intensity is low-to-moderate and the access pattern (especially A[k][i]) tends to be cache-unfriendly, making performance primarily limited by memory bandwidth/cache misses rather than peak compute.

hotspots.location: kernel_trmm(): nested loops i/j/k (inside #pragma scop), approx lines 86-93
hotspots.reason: This is the only O(M^2*N) compute region: for each (i,j) it performs a reduction over k=i+1..M-1 with a multiply-add and updates B[i][j], dominating runtime for realistic sizes.

hotspots.location: print_array(): full scan and fprintf of B, approx lines 49-63 (only if enabled in build/run)
hotspots.estimated_time_percentage: 0-90% (depends on whether PolyBench dump is enabled and output destination)
hotspots.reason: If dumping is enabled, formatted I/O over all elements is extremely slow and can dominate wall time; otherwise it is compiled out or redirected and negligible.
Touches all of A (triangular part) and all of B once; linear in data size and typically small compared to the cubic-ish kernel.

bottleneck_type.reasoning: The kernel performs ~2 flops per inner-k iteration but streams A[k][i] and B[k][j] from memory with poor temporal locality for A (column access in row-major) and limited reuse of B[k][j] across i. Arithmetic intensity is low-to-moderate and the access pattern (especially A[k][i]) tends to be cache-unfriendly, making performance primarily limited by memory bandwidth/cache misses rather than peak compute.

gpu_suitability.reasoning: The kernel is a dense triangular matrix-matrix multiply (TRMM), which maps well to GPUs when implemented with blocked algorithms. However, a naive port of the given loop nest may be bandwidth-limited and suffer from irregular triangular bounds; using a tuned library is key.
gpu_suitability.recommended_approach: Use cuBLAS/rocBLAS TRMM directly (cublasXtrmm/rocblas_trmm). If implementing manually, convert to a blocked GEMM-like approach: process A in tiles, handle triangular structure by masking/choosing tiles, keep B tiles in shared memory, and parallelize over (i,j) tiles with k-tile reductions; apply alpha scaling within the kernel.
